findings.description: The knowledge file states the fully qualified class name `nablarch.core.util.FileRecordWriterHolder`, but the source file never provides the package for this class. Only `FileRecordWriterHolder` (unqualified) appears in the source text. The package `nablarch.core.util` is inferred/guessed, not sourced.
findings.source_evidence: Source uses `FileRecordWriterHolder`_ (RST link reference without package). The only explicit full class name in the source is `nablarch.core.util.FilePathSetting` in the XML example.

findings.description: Section s3 has an empty title and content of "なし" (3 characters, well below the 50-character threshold). This section corresponds to the visual `+++++++++++++++++++` separator lines in the RST source, which are decorative dividers — not a real content section. Creating a knowledge section for a visual separator is unnecessary and adds noise.
findings.source_evidence: The RST source contains `++++++++++++++++++++++` lines as visual separators between the 論理パス and ファイルアクセスAPI blocks. These are not RST headings and carry no content.

findings.description: The class `DataRecord` is used in the code example within s4 (`Map<String, Object> results = new DataRecord();`) but is absent from the s4 hints array.
findings.source_evidence: Source code example: `Map<String, Object> results = new DataRecord();`
